answer:
First, we need to find \( f(4 k-3) \) and \( f(4 k-1) \) for \( k=1,2,...,25 \).

\[
\begin{aligned}
f(1)&=1^{5}-3 \cdot 1^{3}+2 \cdot 1^{2}+3 \cdot 1+6=6 \\
f(2)&=2^{5}-3 \cdot 2^{3}+2 \cdot 2^{2}+3 \cdot 2+6=15 \\
f(3)&=3^{5}-3 \cdot 3^{3}+2 \cdot 3^{2}+3 \cdot 3+6=36 \\
&\vdots \\
f(24)&=24^{5}-3 \cdot 24^{3}+2 \cdot 24^{2}+3 \cdot 24+6=115961 \\
f(25)&=25^{5}-3 \cdot 25^{3}+2 \cdot 25^{2}+3 \cdot 25+6=1664375
\end{aligned}
\]

Now, we calculate \( A_{25} \):

\[
\begin{aligned}
A_{25}&=\prod_{k=1}^{25} \frac{(4 k-3) f(4 k-3)}{(4 k-1) f(4 k-1)} \\
&=\frac{(1 \cdot 6)}{(1 \cdot 15)} \cdot \frac{(5 \cdot 15)}{(3 \cdot 166437)} \cdot \frac{(9 \cdot 36)}{(7 \cdot 115961)} \cdot \frac{(13 \cdot 166437)}{(11 \cdot 166435)} \cdot \frac{(17 \cdot 115961)}{(15 \cdot 115963)} \cdot \frac{(21 \cdot 166435)}{(19 \cdot 166437)} \cdot \frac{(25 \cdot 115963)}{(23 \cdot 115961)} \\
&=\boxed{-\frac{1}{166437}}
\end{aligned}
\]

Note that the negative sign appears due to the alternating nature of the product.
